revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 山直神社 |画像 = [[File: Yamadaijinjya Honden.jpg|260px]]
本殿 (2019年8月) |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[岸和田市]]内畑町3619 |位置 = |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 25 |緯度秒 = 5.7 |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 26 |経度秒 = 9.7 |祭神 = 天照大御神, 天穂日命, 速須佐乃男命 |神格 = |創建年 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |社格 = 村社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 冬宮祭 (2月)
例大祭 (10月) |神事 = |地図 = Japan Osaka }} '''山直神社'''（やまだいじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[岸和田市]]に所在する[[神社]]。本殿等が[[有形文化財]]となっている。 == 歴史・概要 == [[8世紀]]初頭、[[文武天皇]]の時に小角5[[間]]に7間の神殿を造営し、その後[[聖武天皇]]の時代に[[行基]]が奏上して堂塔社殿を造営した。その後[[応仁]]年間に兵火によって焼失したが、[[1595年]] ([[文禄]]3年) に二間四面の神殿を再造営。[[1600年]] ([[慶長]]6年) に屋根[[茅葺き]]を修繕した。{{citeweb|url= http://kamnavi.jp/en/izumi/yamadai.htm |publisher=神奈備|title=山直神社|accessdate=2020-6-20}}。[[延喜式]]内社でかつては長光寺という当社の宮寺を有したが、[[神仏分離]]により分かれた[[1872年]] (明治5年) に[[社格|村社]]に列し、明治44年に八百万神社と杵島神社を合祀した現地案内看板。 ==主祭神== *[[天照大神|天照大御神]] *[[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]] *[[スサノオ|速須佐乃男命]] == 文化財・天然記念物== [[1973年]] ([[昭和]]48年) 、3点が大阪府から文化財指定を受けた。 *本殿 - 1595年建築。[[ヒノキ]]皮葺き、千鳥[[破風]]付。 *石造狛犬　- 一対、[[享和]]4年 *石造灯籠 – [[宝暦]]4年から[[嘉永]]2年 Yamadaijinjya Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Yamadaijinjya Komainu.jpg|狛犬 Yamadaijinjya Toro.jpg|石灯籠 == 現地情報 == ===所在地=== 大阪府岸和田市内畑町3619 ===アクセス=== *[[JR西日本]][[阪和線]][[久米田駅]]からバス沢峯下車 *[[阪和自動車道]][[岸和田和泉インターチェンジ]]より10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[大阪府指定文化財一覧]] * [[和泉国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/site/kishiwada-side/yamadaijinja.html 山直神社] – 岸和田市 *[https://yaokami.jp/1270180/ 山直神社] – 八百万の神 {{DEFAULTSORT:やまたいしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:岸和田市の建築物]] [[Category:岸和田市の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:日本の木造建築物]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]]

{{神社 |名称 = 山直神社 |画像 = [[File: Yamadaijinjya Honden.jpg|250px]]
本殿 |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[岸和田市]]内畑町3619 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[天照大御神]]、[[天穂日命]]、[[スサノオ|速須佐乃男命]] |神格 = |創建年 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |札所等 = |例祭 = 冬宮祭 (2月)
例大祭 (10月) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=山直神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''山直神社'''（やまだいじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[岸和田市]]に所在する[[神社]]。本殿等が[[有形文化財]]となっている。 == 歴史 == [[8世紀]]初頭、[[文武天皇]]の時に小角5[[間]]に7間の神殿を造営し、その後[[聖武天皇]]の時代に[[行基]]が奏上して堂塔社殿を造営した。その後[[応仁]]年間に兵火によって焼失したが、[[1595年]] ([[文禄]]3年) に二間四面の神殿を再造営。[[1600年]] ([[慶長]]6年) に屋根[[茅葺き]]を修繕した。{{Cite web|和書|url= http://kamnavi.jp/en/izumi/yamadai.htm |publisher=神奈備|title=山直神社|accessdate=2020-06-20}}。[[延喜式]]内社でかつては長光寺という当社の宮寺を有したが、[[神仏分離]]により分かれた[[1872年]] (明治5年) に[[社格|村社]]に列し、明治44年に八百万神社と杵島神社を合祀した現地案内看板。 == 祭神 == *[[天照大神|天照大御神]] *[[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]] *[[スサノオ|速須佐乃男命]] == 文化財・天然記念物== [[1973年]] ([[昭和]]48年) 、3点が大阪府から文化財指定を受けた。 *本殿 - 1595年建築。[[ヒノキ]]皮葺き、千鳥[[破風]]付。 *石造狛犬　- 一対、[[享和]]4年 *石造灯籠 – [[宝暦]]4年から[[嘉永]]2年 Yamadaijinjya Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Yamadaijinjya Komainu.jpg|狛犬 Yamadaijinjya Toro.jpg|石灯籠 == 現地情報 == ===所在地=== 大阪府岸和田市内畑町3619 ===アクセス=== *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[阪和線]][[久米田駅]]からバス沢峯下車 *[[阪和自動車道]][[岸和田和泉インターチェンジ]]より10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[大阪府指定文化財一覧]] * [[和泉国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/site/kishiwada-side/yamadaijinja.html 山直神社] – 岸和田市 *[https://yaokami.jp/1270180/ 山直神社] – 八百万の神 {{DEFAULTSORT:やまたいしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:岸和田市の建築物]] [[Category:岸和田市の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:日本の木造建築物]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]]

{{nihongo|'''Yamadai Shrine'''|山直神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Yamadai Shrine'''|山直神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Yamadai Shrine'''|山直神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Yamadai Shrine'''|山直神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Yamadai Shrine'''|山直神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumi Province]].

revision.comment: 5 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 山直神社 |画像 = [[File: Yamadaijinjya Honden.jpg|250px]]
本殿 |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[岸和田市]]内畑町3619 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[天照大御神]]、[[天穂日命]]、[[スサノオ|速須佐乃男命]] |神格 = |創建年 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |札所等 = |例祭 = 冬宮祭 (2月)
例大祭 (10月) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=山直神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''山直神社'''（やまだいじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[岸和田市]]に所在する[[神社]]。本殿等が[[有形文化財]]となっている。 == 歴史 == [[8世紀]]初頭、[[文武天皇]]の時に小角5[[間]]に7間の神殿を造営し、その後[[聖武天皇]]の時代に[[行基]]が奏上して堂塔社殿を造営した。その後[[応仁]]年間に兵火によって焼失したが、[[1595年]] ([[文禄]]3年) に二間四面の神殿を再造営。[[1600年]] ([[慶長]]6年) に屋根[[茅葺き]]を修繕した。{{Cite web|和書|url= http://kamnavi.jp/en/izumi/yamadai.htm |publisher=神奈備|title=山直神社|accessdate=2020-06-20}}。[[延喜式]]内社でかつては長光寺という当社の宮寺を有したが、[[神仏分離]]により分かれた[[1872年]] (明治5年) に[[社格|村社]]に列し、明治44年に八百万神社と杵島神社を合祀した現地案内看板。 == 祭神 == *[[天照大神|天照大御神]] *[[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]] *[[スサノオ|速須佐乃男命]] == 文化財・天然記念物== [[1973年]] ([[昭和]]48年) 、3点が大阪府から文化財指定を受けた。 *本殿 - 1595年建築。[[ヒノキ]]皮葺き、千鳥[[破風]]付。 *石造狛犬　- 一対、[[享和]]4年 *石造灯籠 – [[宝暦]]4年から[[嘉永]]2年 Yamadaijinjya Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Yamadaijinjya Komainu.jpg|狛犬 Yamadaijinjya Toro.jpg|石灯籠 == 現地情報 == ===所在地=== 大阪府岸和田市内畑町3619 ===アクセス=== *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[阪和線]][[久米田駅]]からバス沢峯下車 *[[阪和自動車道]][[岸和田和泉インターチェンジ]]より10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[大阪府指定文化財一覧]] * [[和泉国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/site/kishiwada-side/yamadaijinja.html 山直神社] – 岸和田市 *[https://yaokami.jp/1270180/ 山直神社] – 八百万の神 {{DEFAULTSORT:やまたいしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:岸和田市の建築物]] [[Category:岸和田市の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:日本の木造建築物]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]]

[[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|山直神社|version=107249300|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 山直神社 |画像 = [[File: Yamadaijinjya Honden.jpg|250px]]
本殿 |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[岸和田市]]内畑町3619 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[天照大御神]]、[[天穂日命]]、[[スサノオ|速須佐乃男命]] |神格 = |創建年 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |札所等 = |例祭 = 冬宮祭 (2月)
例大祭 (10月) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=山直神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''山直神社'''（やまだいじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[岸和田市]]に所在する[[神社]]。本殿等が[[有形文化財]]となっている。 == 歴史 == [[8世紀]]初頭、[[文武天皇]]の時に小角5[[間]]に7間の神殿を造営し、その後[[聖武天皇]]の時代に[[行基]]が奏上して堂塔社殿を造営した。その後[[応仁]]年間に兵火によって焼失したが、[[1595年]] ([[文禄]]3年) に二間四面の神殿を再造営。[[1600年]] ([[慶長]]6年) に屋根[[茅葺き]]を修繕した。{{Cite web|和書|url= http://kamnavi.jp/en/izumi/yamadai.htm |publisher=神奈備|title=山直神社|accessdate=2020-06-20}}。[[延喜式]]内社でかつては長光寺という当社の宮寺を有したが、[[神仏分離]]により分かれた[[1872年]] (明治5年) に[[社格|村社]]に列し、明治44年に八百万神社と杵島神社を合祀した現地案内看板。 == 祭神 == *[[天照大神|天照大御神]] *[[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]] *[[スサノオ|速須佐乃男命]] == 文化財・天然記念物== [[1973年]] ([[昭和]]48年) 、3点が大阪府から文化財指定を受けた。 *本殿 - 1595年建築。[[ヒノキ]]皮葺き、千鳥[[破風]]付。 *石造狛犬　- 一対、[[享和]]4年 *石造灯籠 – [[宝暦]]4年から[[嘉永]]2年 Yamadaijinjya Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Yamadaijinjya Komainu.jpg|狛犬 Yamadaijinjya Toro.jpg|石灯籠 == 現地情報 == ===所在地=== 大阪府岸和田市内畑町3619 ===アクセス=== *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[阪和線]][[久米田駅]]からバス沢峯下車 *[[阪和自動車道]][[岸和田和泉インターチェンジ]]より10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[大阪府指定文化財一覧]] * [[和泉国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/site/kishiwada-side/yamadaijinja.html 山直神社] – 岸和田市 *[https://yaokami.jp/1270180/ 山直神社] – 八百万の神 {{DEFAULTSORT:やまたいしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:岸和田市の建築物]] [[Category:岸和田市の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:日本の木造建築物]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]] ==End Japanese==

[[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|山直神社|version=107249300|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 山直神社 |画像 = [[File: Yamadaijinjya Honden.jpg|250px]]
本殿 |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[岸和田市]]内畑町3619 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[天照大御神]]、[[天穂日命]]、[[スサノオ|速須佐乃男命]] |神格 = |創建年 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |札所等 = |例祭 = 冬宮祭 (2月)
例大祭 (10月) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=山直神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''山直神社'''（やまだいじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[岸和田市]]に所在する[[神社]]。本殿等が[[有形文化財]]となっている。 == 歴史 == [[8世紀]]初頭、[[文武天皇]]の時に小角5[[間]]に7間の神殿を造営し、その後[[聖武天皇]]の時代に[[行基]]が奏上して堂塔社殿を造営した。その後[[応仁]]年間に兵火によって焼失したが、[[1595年]] ([[文禄]]3年) に二間四面の神殿を再造営。[[1600年]] ([[慶長]]6年) に屋根[[茅葺き]]を修繕した。{{Cite web|和書|url= http://kamnavi.jp/en/izumi/yamadai.htm |publisher=神奈備|title=山直神社|accessdate=2020-06-20}}。[[延喜式]]内社でかつては長光寺という当社の宮寺を有したが、[[神仏分離]]により分かれた[[1872年]] (明治5年) に[[社格|村社]]に列し、明治44年に八百万神社と杵島神社を合祀した現地案内看板。 == 祭神 == *[[天照大神|天照大御神]] *[[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]] *[[スサノオ|速須佐乃男命]] == 文化財・天然記念物== [[1973年]] ([[昭和]]48年) 、3点が大阪府から文化財指定を受けた。 *本殿 - 1595年建築。[[ヒノキ]]皮葺き、千鳥[[破風]]付。 *石造狛犬　- 一対、[[享和]]4年 *石造灯籠 – [[宝暦]]4年から[[嘉永]]2年 Yamadaijinjya Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Yamadaijinjya Komainu.jpg|狛犬 Yamadaijinjya Toro.jpg|石灯籠 == 現地情報 == ===所在地=== 大阪府岸和田市内畑町3619 ===アクセス=== *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[阪和線]][[久米田駅]]からバス沢峯下車 *[[阪和自動車道]][[岸和田和泉インターチェンジ]]より10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[大阪府指定文化財一覧]] * [[和泉国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/site/kishiwada-side/yamadaijinja.html 山直神社] – 岸和田市 *[https://yaokami.jp/1270180/ 山直神社] – 八百万の神 [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:岸和田市の建築物]] [[Category:岸和田市の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:日本の木造建築物]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]] ==End Japanese==

[[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|山直神社|version=107249300|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 山直神社 |画像 = [[File: Yamadaijinjya Honden.jpg|250px]]
本殿 |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[岸和田市]]内畑町3619 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[天照大御神]]、[[天穂日命]]、[[スサノオ|速須佐乃男命]] |神格 = |創建年 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |札所等 = |例祭 = 冬宮祭 (2月)
例大祭 (10月) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=山直神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''山直神社'''（やまだいじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[岸和田市]]に所在する[[神社]]。本殿等が[[有形文化財]]となっている。 == 歴史 == [[8世紀]]初頭、[[文武天皇]]の時に小角5[[間]]に7間の神殿を造営し、その後[[聖武天皇]]の時代に[[行基]]が奏上して堂塔社殿を造営した。その後[[応仁]]年間に兵火によって焼失したが、[[1595年]] ([[文禄]]3年) に二間四面の神殿を再造営。[[1600年]] ([[慶長]]6年) に屋根[[茅葺き]]を修繕した。{{Cite web|和書|url= http://kamnavi.jp/en/izumi/yamadai.htm |publisher=神奈備|title=山直神社|accessdate=2020-06-20}}。[[延喜式]]内社でかつては長光寺という当社の宮寺を有したが、[[神仏分離]]により分かれた[[1872年]] (明治5年) に[[社格|村社]]に列し、明治44年に八百万神社と杵島神社を合祀した現地案内看板。 == 祭神 == *[[天照大神|天照大御神]] *[[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]] *[[スサノオ|速須佐乃男命]] == 文化財・天然記念物== [[1973年]] ([[昭和]]48年) 、3点が大阪府から文化財指定を受けた。 *本殿 - 1595年建築。[[ヒノキ]]皮葺き、千鳥[[破風]]付。 *石造狛犬　- 一対、[[享和]]4年 *石造灯籠 – [[宝暦]]4年から[[嘉永]]2年 Yamadaijinjya Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Yamadaijinjya Komainu.jpg|狛犬 Yamadaijinjya Toro.jpg|石灯籠 == 現地情報 == ===所在地=== 大阪府岸和田市内畑町3619 ===アクセス=== *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[阪和線]][[久米田駅]]からバス沢峯下車 *[[阪和自動車道]][[岸和田和泉インターチェンジ]]より10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[大阪府指定文化財一覧]] * [[和泉国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/site/kishiwada-side/yamadaijinja.html 山直神社] – 岸和田市 *[https://yaokami.jp/1270180/ 山直神社] – 八百万の神 ==End Japanese== [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:岸和田市の建築物]] [[Category:岸和田市の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:日本の木造建築物]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]]

[[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|山直神社|version=107249300|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 山直神社 |画像 = [[File: Yamadaijinjya Honden.jpg|250px]]
本殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|岸和田市|Q740456}}内畑町3619 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|天照大御神}}、{{jalink|天穂日命}}、{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|速須佐乃男命}} |神格 = |創建年 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|村社}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 冬宮祭 (2月)
例大祭 (10月) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=山直神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''山直神社'''（やまだいじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|岸和田市|Q740456}}に所在する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。本殿等が{{jalink|有形文化財|Q2901860}}となっている。 == 歴史 == {{jalink|8世紀|Q8086}}初頭、{{jalink|文武天皇|Q357246}}の時に小角5{{jalink|間|Q3195007}}に7間の神殿を造営し、その後{{jalink|聖武天皇|Q349263}}の時代に{{jalink|行基|Q1136664}}が奏上して堂塔社殿を造営した。その後{{jalink|応仁|Q1143903}}年間に兵火によって焼失したが、{{jalink|1595年|Q6782}} ({{jalink|文禄|Q1373039}}3年) に二間四面の神殿を再造営。{{jalink|1600年|Q6720}} ({{jalink|慶長|Q1328185}}6年) に屋根{{jalink|茅葺き}}を修繕した。{{Cite web|和書|url= http://kamnavi.jp/en/izumi/yamadai.htm |publisher=神奈備|title=山直神社|accessdate=2020-06-20}}。{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}内社でかつては長光寺という当社の宮寺を有したが、{{jalink|神仏分離|Q1970535}}により分かれた{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}} (明治5年) に{{jalink|社格|Q10444029|村社}}に列し、明治44年に八百万神社と杵島神社を合祀した現地案内看板。 == 祭神 == *{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照大御神}} *{{jalink|アメノホヒ|Q10940685|天穂日命}} *{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|速須佐乃男命}} == 文化財・天然記念物== {{jalink|1973年|Q2477}} ({{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}48年) 、3点が大阪府から文化財指定を受けた。 *本殿 - 1595年建築。{{jalink|ヒノキ|Q917187}}皮葺き、千鳥{{jalink|破風|Q4881511}}付。 *石造狛犬　- 一対、{{jalink|享和|Q960763}}4年 *石造灯籠 – {{jalink|宝暦|Q1194397}}4年から{{jalink|嘉永|Q1146125}}2年 Yamadaijinjya Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Yamadaijinjya Komainu.jpg|狛犬 Yamadaijinjya Toro.jpg|石灯籠 == 現地情報 == ===所在地=== 大阪府岸和田市内畑町3619 ===アクセス=== *{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125|JR西日本}}{{jalink|阪和線|Q1154800}}{{jalink|久米田駅|Q857003}}からバス沢峯下車 *{{jalink|阪和自動車道|Q1872998}}{{jalink|岸和田和泉インターチェンジ|Q11051119}}より10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * {{jalink|大阪府指定文化財一覧|Q11440963}} * {{jalink|和泉国の式内社一覧|Q11417778}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/site/kishiwada-side/yamadaijinja.html 山直神社] – 岸和田市 *[https://yaokami.jp/1270180/ 山直神社] – 八百万の神 ==End Japanese== [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:岸和田市の建築物]] [[Category:岸和田市の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:日本の木造建築物]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]]

1595年建築。{{jalink|ヒノキ|Q917187}}皮葺き、千鳥{{jalink|破風|Q4881511}}付。 *石造狛犬　- 一対、{{jalink|享和|Q960763}}4年 *石造灯籠 – {{jalink|宝暦|Q1194397}}4年から{{jalink|嘉永|Q1146125}}2年 Yamadaijinjya Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Yamadaijinjya Komainu.jpg|狛犬 Yamadaijinjya Toro.jpg|石灯籠 === 現地情報 === ===所在地=== 大阪府岸和田市内畑町3619 ===アクセス=== *{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125|JR西日本}}{{jalink|阪和線|Q1154800}}{{jalink|久米田駅|Q857003}}からバス沢峯下車 *{{jalink|阪和自動車道|Q1872998}}{{jalink|岸和田和泉インターチェンジ|Q11051119}}より10分 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|大阪府指定文化財一覧|Q11440963}} * {{jalink|和泉国の式内社一覧|Q11417778}} === 外部リンク === * [https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/site/kishiwada-side/yamadaijinja.html 山直神社] – 岸和田市 *[https://yaokami.jp/1270180/ 山直神社] – 八百万の神 {{DEFAULTSORT:やまたいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:岸和田市の建築物]] [[Category:岸和田市の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:日本の木造建築物]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]]

It is located in [[Izumi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần district|ja|和泉郡|zh|和泉郡|WD=Q10982247}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.418299|135.436027}} == image (P18) == * Yamadaijinjya Honden.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yamadai-jinja}} [[JA:山直神社]] {{wikidata link|Q97276772}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 山直神社 |画像 = [[File: Yamadaijinjya Honden.jpg|250px]]
本殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|岸和田市|Q740456}}内畑町3619 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|天照大御神}}、{{jalink|天穂日命}}、{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|速須佐乃男命}} |神格 = |創建年 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 冬宮祭 (2月)
例大祭 (10月) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=山直神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''山直神社'''（やまだいじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|岸和田市|Q740456}}に所在する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。本殿等が{{jalink|有形文化財|Q2901860}}となっている。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|8世紀|Q8086}}初頭、{{jalink|文武天皇|Q357246}}の時に小角5{{jalink|間|Q3195007}}に7間の神殿を造営し、その後{{jalink|聖武天皇|Q349263}}の時代に{{jalink|行基|Q1136664}}が奏上して堂塔社殿を造営した。その後{{jalink|応仁|Q1143903}}年間に兵火によって焼失したが、{{jalink|1595年|Q6782}} ({{jalink|文禄|Q1373039}}3年) に二間四面の神殿を再造営。{{jalink|1600年|Q6720}} ({{jalink|慶長|Q1328185}}6年) に屋根{{jalink|茅葺き}}を修繕した。{{Cite web|和書|url= http://kamnavi.jp/en/izumi/yamadai.htm |publisher=神奈備|title=山直神社|accessdate=2020-06-20}}。{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}内社でかつては長光寺という当社の宮寺を有したが、{{jalink|神仏分離|Q1970535}}により分かれた{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}} (明治5年) に{{jalink|社格|Q10444029|村社}}に列し、明治44年に八百万神社と杵島神社を合祀した現地案内看板。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照大御神}} *{{jalink|アメノホヒ|Q10940685|天穂日命}} *{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|速須佐乃男命}} === 文化財・天然記念物=== {{jalink|1973年|Q2477}} ({{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}48年) 、3点が大阪府から文化財指定を受けた。 *本殿 - 1595年建築。{{jalink|ヒノキ|Q917187}}皮葺き、千鳥{{jalink|破風|Q4881511}}付。 *石造狛犬　- 一対、{{jalink|享和|Q960763}}4年 *石造灯籠 – {{jalink|宝暦|Q1194397}}4年から{{jalink|嘉永|Q1146125}}2年 Yamadaijinjya Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Yamadaijinjya Komainu.jpg|狛犬 Yamadaijinjya Toro.jpg|石灯籠 === 現地情報 === ===所在地=== 大阪府岸和田市内畑町3619 ===アクセス=== *{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125|JR西日本}}{{jalink|阪和線|Q1154800}}{{jalink|久米田駅|Q857003}}からバス沢峯下車 *{{jalink|阪和自動車道|Q1872998}}{{jalink|岸和田和泉インターチェンジ|Q11051119}}より10分 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|大阪府指定文化財一覧|Q11440963}} * {{jalink|和泉国の式内社一覧|Q11417778}} === 外部リンク === * [https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/site/kishiwada-side/yamadaijinja.html 山直神社] – 岸和田市 *[https://yaokami.jp/1270180/ 山直神社] – 八百万の神 {{DEFAULTSORT:やまたいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamadai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:岸和田市の建築物]] [[Category:岸和田市の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:日本の木造建築物]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]] [[Category:need translation]]

例大祭 (10月) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=山直神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''山直神社'''（やまだいじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|岸和田市|Q740456}}に所在する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。本殿等が{{jalink|有形文化財|Q2901860}}となっている。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|8世紀|Q8086}}初頭、{{jalink|文武天皇|Q357246}}の時に小角5{{jalink|間|Q3195007}}に7間の神殿を造営し、その後{{jalink|聖武天皇|Q349263}}の時代に{{jalink|行基|Q1136664}}が奏上して堂塔社殿を造営した。その後{{jalink|応仁|Q1143903}}年間に兵火によって焼失したが、{{jalink|1595年|Q6782}} ({{jalink|文禄|Q1373039}}3年) に二間四面の神殿を再造営。{{jalink|1600年|Q6720}} ({{jalink|慶長|Q1328185}}6年) に屋根{{jalink|茅葺き}}を修繕した。{{Cite web|和書|url= http://kamnavi.jp/en/izumi/yamadai.htm |publisher=神奈備|title=山直神社|accessdate=2020-06-20}}。{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}内社でかつては長光寺という当社の宮寺を有したが、{{jalink|神仏分離|Q1970535}}により分かれた{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}} (明治5年) に{{jalink|社格|Q10444029|村社}}に列し、明治44年に八百万神社と杵島神社を合祀した現地案内看板。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照大御神}} *{{jalink|アメノホヒ|Q10940685|天穂日命}} *{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|速須佐乃男命}} === 文化財・天然記念物=== {{jalink|1973年|Q2477}} ({{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}48年) 、3点が大阪府から文化財指定を受けた。 *本殿 - 1595年建築。{{jalink|ヒノキ|Q917187}}皮葺き、千鳥{{jalink|破風|Q4881511}}付。 *石造狛犬　- 一対、{{jalink|享和|Q960763}}4年 *石造灯籠 – {{jalink|宝暦|Q1194397}}4年から{{jalink|嘉永|Q1146125}}2年 Yamadaijinjya Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Yamadaijinjya Komainu.jpg|狛犬 Yamadaijinjya Toro.jpg|石灯籠 === 現地情報 === ===所在地=== 大阪府岸和田市内畑町3619 ===アクセス=== *{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125|JR西日本}}{{jalink|阪和線|Q1154800}}{{jalink|久米田駅|Q857003}}からバス沢峯下車 *{{jalink|阪和自動車道|Q1872998}}{{jalink|岸和田和泉インターチェンジ|Q11051119}}より10分 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|大阪府指定文化財一覧|Q11440963}} * {{jalink|和泉国の式内社一覧|Q11417778}} === 外部リンク === * [https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/site/kishiwada-side/yamadaijinja.html 山直神社] – 岸和田市 *[https://yaokami.jp/1270180/ 山直神社] – 八百万の神 {{DEFAULTSORT:やまたいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamadai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamadai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:岸和田市の建築物]] [[Category:岸和田市の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:日本の木造建築物]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]] [[Category:need translation]]

It is located in [[Izumi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yamadai-jinja}} [[JA:山直神社]] {{wikidata link|Q97276772}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yamadai Shrine |画像 = [[File: Yamadaijinjya Honden.jpg|250px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Kishiwada|Q740456}}Uchihata-chō 3619 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami}}、{{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}}、{{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}} |神格 = |創建年 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |札所等 = |例祭 = Winter shrine festival (February)
Grand annual festival (October) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Yamadai Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yamadai Shrine''' (やまだいじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kishiwada|Q740456}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. Its honden and other structures are designated {{jalink|tangible cultural properties|Q2901860}}. === History === In the early {{jalink|8th century|Q8086}}, in the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Monmu|Q357246}}, a shrine hall of 7 bays was built with a small corner width of 5 {{jalink|ken|Q3195007}}, and later in the age of {{jalink|Emperor Shōmu|Q349263}}, {{jalink|Gyōki|Q1136664}} petitioned and temple halls and shrine buildings were constructed. The site was later destroyed by war fire in the {{jalink|Ōnin|Q1143903}} era, but in {{jalink|1595|Q6782}} ({{jalink|Bunroku|Q1373039}} 3) a two-by-four-bay shrine hall was rebuilt. In {{jalink|1600|Q6720}} ({{jalink|Keichō|Q1328185}} 6), its thatched roof was repaired.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://kamnavi.jp/en/izumi/yamadai.htm |publisher=Kannabi|title=Yamadai Shrine|accessdate=2020-06-20}}. It was an Engishiki inner shrine and once had a temple-shrine of this sanctuary called Chōkō-ji, but was separated by {{jalink|shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535}}. In {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked {{jalink|village shrine|Q10444029|village shrine}}, and in Meiji 44 it merged Yaoyorozu Shrine and Kijima ShrineLocal information signboard. === Enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu Ōmikami}} *{{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties / Natural monuments === In {{jalink|1973|Q2477}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 48), three items were designated cultural properties by Osaka Prefecture. *Honden - built in 1595. {{jalink|Japanese cypress|Q917187}} bark roof, with chidori {{jalink|hafu gable|Q4881511}}. *Stone komainu - one pair, {{jalink|Kyōwa|Q960763}} 4 *Stone lanterns - from {{jalink|Hōreki|Q1194397}} 4 to {{jalink|Kaei|Q1146125}} 2 Yamadaijinjya Haiden.jpg|Haiden Yamadaijinjya Komainu.jpg|Komainu Yamadaijinjya Toro.jpg|Stone lantern === Local information === ===Location=== Uchihata-chō 3619, Kishiwada, Osaka ===Access=== *From {{jalink|Kumeda Station|Q857003}} on {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Hanwa Line|Q1154800}}, take bus and get off at Sawamine *10 minutes from {{jalink|Kishiwada-Izumi Interchange|Q11051119}} on the {{jalink|Hanwa Expressway|Q1872998}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|List of Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural properties|Q11440963}} * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province|Q11417778}} === External links === * [https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/site/kishiwada-side/yamadaijinja.html Yamadai Shrine] - Kishiwada City *[https://yaokami.jp/1270180/ Yamadai Shrine] - Yaokami {{DEFAULTSORT:やまたいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamadai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamadai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:岸和田市の建築物]] [[Category:岸和田市の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:日本の木造建築物]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]] [[Category:need translation]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yamadai-jinja}} [[JA:山直神社]] {{wikidata link|Q97276772}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yamadai Shrine |画像 = [[File: Yamadaijinjya Honden.jpg|250px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Kishiwada|Q740456}}Uchihata-chō 3619 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami}}、{{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}}、{{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}} |神格 = |創建年 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |札所等 = |例祭 = Winter shrine festival (February)
Grand annual festival (October) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Yamadai Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yamadai Shrine''' (やまだいじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kishiwada|Q740456}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. Its honden and other structures are designated {{jalink|tangible cultural properties|Q2901860}}. === History === In the early {{jalink|8th century|Q8086}}, in the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Monmu|Q357246}}, a shrine hall of 7 bays was built with a small corner width of 5 {{jalink|ken|Q3195007}}, and later in the age of {{jalink|Emperor Shōmu|Q349263}}, {{jalink|Gyōki|Q1136664}} petitioned and temple halls and shrine buildings were constructed. The site was later destroyed by war fire in the {{jalink|Ōnin|Q1143903}} era, but in {{jalink|1595|Q6782}} ({{jalink|Bunroku|Q1373039}} 3) a two-by-four-bay shrine hall was rebuilt. In {{jalink|1600|Q6720}} ({{jalink|Keichō|Q1328185}} 6), its thatched roof was repaired.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://kamnavi.jp/en/izumi/yamadai.htm |publisher=Kannabi|title=Yamadai Shrine|accessdate=2020-06-20}}. It was an Engishiki inner shrine and once had a temple-shrine of this sanctuary called Chōkō-ji, but was separated by {{jalink|shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535}}. In {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked {{jalink|village shrine|Q10444029|village shrine}}, and in Meiji 44 it merged Yaoyorozu Shrine and Kijima ShrineLocal information signboard. === Enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu Ōmikami}} *{{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties / Natural monuments === In {{jalink|1973|Q2477}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 48), three items were designated cultural properties by Osaka Prefecture. *Honden - built in 1595. {{jalink|Japanese cypress|Q917187}} bark roof, with chidori {{jalink|hafu gable|Q4881511}}. *Stone komainu - one pair, {{jalink|Kyōwa|Q960763}} 4 *Stone lanterns - from {{jalink|Hōreki|Q1194397}} 4 to {{jalink|Kaei|Q1146125}} 2 Yamadaijinjya Haiden.jpg|Haiden Yamadaijinjya Komainu.jpg|Komainu Yamadaijinjya Toro.jpg|Stone lantern === Local information === ===Location=== Uchihata-chō 3619, Kishiwada, Osaka ===Access=== *From {{jalink|Kumeda Station|Q857003}} on {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Hanwa Line|Q1154800}}, take bus and get off at Sawamine *10 minutes from {{jalink|Kishiwada-Izumi Interchange|Q11051119}} on the {{jalink|Hanwa Expressway|Q1872998}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|List of Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural properties|Q11440963}} * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province|Q11417778}} === External links === * [https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/site/kishiwada-side/yamadaijinja.html Yamadai Shrine] - Kishiwada City *[https://yaokami.jp/1270180/ Yamadai Shrine] - Yaokami {{DEFAULTSORT:やまたいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamadai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamadai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:岸和田市の建築物]] [[Category:岸和田市の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:日本の木造建築物]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]] [[Category:need translation]]

Honden |所在地 = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Kishiwada|Q740456}}Uchihata-chō 3619 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami}}、{{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}}、{{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}} |神格 = |創建年 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |札所等 = |例祭 = Winter shrine festival (February)
Grand annual festival (October) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Yamadai Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yamadai Shrine''' (やまだいじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kishiwada|Q740456}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. Its honden and other structures are designated {{jalink|tangible cultural properties|Q2901860}}. === History === In the early {{jalink|8th century|Q8086}}, in the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Monmu|Q357246}}, a shrine hall of 7 bays was built with a small corner width of 5 {{jalink|ken|Q3195007}}, and later in the age of {{jalink|Emperor Shōmu|Q349263}}, {{jalink|Gyōki|Q1136664}} petitioned and temple halls and shrine buildings were constructed. The site was later destroyed by war fire in the {{jalink|Ōnin|Q1143903}} era, but in {{jalink|1595|Q6782}} ({{jalink|Bunroku|Q1373039}} 3) a two-by-four-bay shrine hall was rebuilt. In {{jalink|1600|Q6720}} ({{jalink|Keichō|Q1328185}} 6), its thatched roof was repaired.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://kamnavi.jp/en/izumi/yamadai.htm |publisher=Kannabi|title=Yamadai Shrine|accessdate=2020-06-20}}. It was an Engishiki inner shrine and once had a temple-shrine of this sanctuary called Chōkō-ji, but was separated by {{jalink|shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535}}. In {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked {{jalink|village shrine|Q10444029|village shrine}}, and in Meiji 44 it merged Yaoyorozu Shrine and Kijima ShrineLocal information signboard. === Enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu Ōmikami}} *{{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties / Natural monuments === In {{jalink|1973|Q2477}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 48), three items were designated cultural properties by Osaka Prefecture. *Honden - built in 1595. {{jalink|Japanese cypress|Q917187}} bark roof, with chidori {{jalink|hafu gable|Q4881511}}. *Stone komainu - one pair, {{jalink|Kyōwa|Q960763}} 4 *Stone lanterns - from {{jalink|Hōreki|Q1194397}} 4 to {{jalink|Kaei|Q1146125}} 2 Yamadaijinjya Haiden.jpg|Haiden Yamadaijinjya Komainu.jpg|Komainu Yamadaijinjya Toro.jpg|Stone lantern === Local information === ===Location=== Uchihata-chō 3619, Kishiwada, Osaka ===Access=== *From {{jalink|Kumeda Station|Q857003}} on {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Hanwa Line|Q1154800}}, take bus and get off at Sawamine *10 minutes from {{jalink|Kishiwada-Izumi Interchange|Q11051119}} on the {{jalink|Hanwa Expressway|Q1872998}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|List of Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural properties|Q11440963}} * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province|Q11417778}} === External links === * [https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/site/kishiwada-side/yamadaijinja.html Yamadai Shrine] - Grand annual festival (October) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Yamadai Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yamadai Shrine''' (やまだいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kishiwada|Q740456|WD=Q740456}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. Its honden and other structures are designated {{ill|lt=tangible cultural properties|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860}}. === History === In the early {{ill|lt=8th century|Q8086|WD=Q8086}}, in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Monmu|Q357246|WD=Q357246}}, a shrine hall of 7 bays was built with a small corner width of 5 {{ill|lt=ken|Q3195007|WD=Q3195007}}, and later in the age of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263}}, {{ill|lt=Gyōki|Q1136664|WD=Q1136664}} petitioned and temple halls and shrine buildings were constructed. The site was later destroyed by war fire in the {{ill|lt=Ōnin|Q1143903|WD=Q1143903}} era, but in {{ill|lt=1595|Q6782|WD=Q6782}} ({{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039}} 3) a two-by-four-bay shrine hall was rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 6), its thatched roof was repaired.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://kamnavi.jp/en/izumi/yamadai.htm |publisher=Kannabi|title=Yamadai Shrine|accessdate=2020-06-20}}. It was an Engishiki inner shrine and once had a temple-shrine of this sanctuary called Chōkō-ji, but was separated by {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|lt=village shrine}}, and in Meiji 44 it merged Yaoyorozu Shrine and Kijima ShrineLocal information signboard. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties / Natural monuments === In {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 48), three items were designated cultural properties by Osaka Prefecture. *Honden - built in 1595. {{ill|lt=Japanese cypress|Q917187|WD=Q917187}} bark roof, with chidori {{ill|lt=hafu gable|Q4881511|WD=Q4881511}}. *Stone komainu - one pair, {{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763}} 4 *Stone lanterns - from {{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397}} 4 to {{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125}} 2 Yamadaijinjya Haiden.jpg|Haiden Yamadaijinjya Komainu.jpg|Komainu Yamadaijinjya Toro.jpg|Stone lantern === Local information === ===Location=== Uchihata-chō 3619, Kishiwada, Osaka ===Access=== *From {{ill|lt=Kumeda Station|Q857003|WD=Q857003}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Hanwa Line|Q1154800|WD=Q1154800}}, take bus and get off at Sawamine *10 minutes from {{ill|lt=Kishiwada-Izumi Interchange|Q11051119|WD=Q11051119}} on the {{ill|lt=Hanwa Expressway|Q1872998|WD=Q1872998}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural properties|Q11440963|WD=Q11440963}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province|Q11417778|WD=Q11417778}} === External links === * [https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/site/kishiwada-side/yamadaijinja.html Yamadai Shrine] - Kishiwada City *[https://yaokami.jp/1270180/ Yamadai Shrine] - Yaokami {{DEFAULTSORT:やまたいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamadai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamadai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:岸和田市の建築物]] [[Category:岸和田市の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:日本の木造建築物]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is located in [[Izumi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Kishiwada|Q740456|WD=Q740456}}Uchihata-chō 3619 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}} |神格 = |創建年 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |札所等 = |例祭 = Winter shrine festival (February)
Grand annual festival (October) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Yamadai Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yamadai Shrine''' (やまだいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kishiwada|Q740456|WD=Q740456}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. Its honden and other structures are designated {{ill|lt=tangible cultural properties|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860}}. === History === In the early {{ill|lt=8th century|Q8086|WD=Q8086}}, in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Monmu|Q357246|WD=Q357246}}, a shrine hall of 7 bays was built with a small corner width of 5 {{ill|lt=ken|Q3195007|WD=Q3195007}}, and later in the age of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263}}, {{ill|lt=Gyōki|Q1136664|WD=Q1136664}} petitioned and temple halls and shrine buildings were constructed. The site was later destroyed by war fire in the {{ill|lt=Ōnin|Q1143903|WD=Q1143903}} era, but in {{ill|lt=1595|Q6782|WD=Q6782}} ({{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039}} 3) a two-by-four-bay shrine hall was rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 6), its thatched roof was repaired.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://kamnavi.jp/en/izumi/yamadai.htm |publisher=Kannabi|title=Yamadai Shrine|accessdate=2020-06-20}}. It was an Engishiki inner shrine and once had a temple-shrine of this sanctuary called Chōkō-ji, but was separated by {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|lt=village shrine}}, and in Meiji 44 it merged Yaoyorozu Shrine and Kijima ShrineLocal information signboard. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties / Natural monuments === In {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 48), three items were designated cultural properties by Osaka Prefecture. *Honden - built in 1595. {{ill|lt=Japanese cypress|Q917187|WD=Q917187}} bark roof, with chidori {{ill|lt=hafu gable|Q4881511|WD=Q4881511}}. *Stone komainu - one pair, {{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763}} 4 *Stone lanterns - from {{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397}} 4 to {{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125}} 2 Yamadaijinjya Haiden.jpg|Haiden Yamadaijinjya Komainu.jpg|Komainu Yamadaijinjya Toro.jpg|Stone lantern === Local information === ===Location=== Uchihata-chō 3619, Kishiwada, Osaka ===Access=== *From {{ill|lt=Kumeda Station|Q857003|WD=Q857003}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Hanwa Line|Q1154800|WD=Q1154800}}, take bus and get off at Sawamine *10 minutes from {{ill|lt=Kishiwada-Izumi Interchange|Q11051119|WD=Q11051119}} on the {{ill|lt=Hanwa Expressway|Q1872998|WD=Q1872998}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural properties|Q11440963|WD=Q11440963}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province|Q11417778|WD=Q11417778}} === External links === * [https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/site/kishiwada-side/yamadaijinja.html Yamadai Shrine] - Kishiwada City *[https://yaokami.jp/1270180/ Yamadai Shrine] - Yaokami {{DEFAULTSORT:やまたいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamadai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamadai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:岸和田市の建築物]] [[Category:岸和田市の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:日本の木造建築物]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Kishiwada|Q740456|WD=Q740456}}Uchihata-chō 3619 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q455602}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}} |神格 = |創建年 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |札所等 = |例祭 = Winter shrine festival (February)
Grand annual festival (October) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Yamadai Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yamadai Shrine''' (やまだいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kishiwada|Q740456|WD=Q740456}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. Its honden and other structures are designated {{ill|lt=tangible cultural properties|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860}}. === History === In the early {{ill|lt=8th century|Q8086|WD=Q8086}}, in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Monmu|Q357246|WD=Q357246}}, a shrine hall of 7 bays was built with a small corner width of 5 {{ill|lt=ken|Q3195007|WD=Q3195007}}, and later in the age of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263}}, {{ill|lt=Gyōki|Q1136664|WD=Q1136664}} petitioned and temple halls and shrine buildings were constructed. The site was later destroyed by war fire in the {{ill|lt=Ōnin|Q1143903|WD=Q1143903}} era, but in {{ill|lt=1595|Q6782|WD=Q6782}} ({{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039}} 3) a two-by-four-bay shrine hall was rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 6), its thatched roof was repaired.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://kamnavi.jp/en/izumi/yamadai.htm |publisher=Kannabi|title=Yamadai Shrine|accessdate=2020-06-20}}. It was an Engishiki inner shrine and once had a temple-shrine of this sanctuary called Chōkō-ji, but was separated by {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|lt=village shrine}}, and in Meiji 44 it merged Yaoyorozu Shrine and Kijima ShrineLocal information signboard. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties / Natural monuments === In {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 48), three items were designated cultural properties by Osaka Prefecture. *Honden - built in 1595. {{ill|lt=Japanese cypress|Q917187|WD=Q917187}} bark roof, with chidori {{ill|lt=hafu gable|Q4881511|WD=Q4881511}}. *Stone komainu - one pair, {{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763}} 4 *Stone lanterns - from {{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397}} 4 to {{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125}} 2 Yamadaijinjya Haiden.jpg|Haiden Yamadaijinjya Komainu.jpg|Komainu Yamadaijinjya Toro.jpg|Stone lantern === Local information === ===Location=== Uchihata-chō 3619, Kishiwada, Osaka ===Access=== *From {{ill|lt=Kumeda Station|Q857003|WD=Q857003}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Hanwa Line|Q1154800|WD=Q1154800}}, take bus and get off at Sawamine *10 minutes from {{ill|lt=Kishiwada-Izumi Interchange|Q11051119|WD=Q11051119}} on the {{ill|lt=Hanwa Expressway|Q1872998|WD=Q1872998}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural properties|Q11440963|WD=Q11440963}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province|Q11417778|WD=Q11417778}} === External links === * [https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/site/kishiwada-side/yamadaijinja.html Yamadai Shrine] - Kishiwada City *[https://yaokami.jp/1270180/ Yamadai Shrine] - Yaokami {{DEFAULTSORT:やまたいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamadai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamadai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:岸和田市の建築物]] [[Category:岸和田市の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:日本の木造建築物]] [[Category:安土桃山時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|lt=village shrine|1=Shakaku}}, and in Meiji 44 it merged Yaoyorozu Shrine and Kijima ShrineLocal information signboard. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties / Natural monuments === In {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477|1=1973}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 48), three items were designated cultural properties by Osaka Prefecture. *Honden - built in 1595. {{ill|lt=Japanese cypress|Q917187|WD=Q917187|1=Chamaecyparis obtusa}} bark roof, with chidori {{ill|lt=hafu gable|Q4881511|WD=Q4881511|1=Bargeboard}}. *Stone komainu - one pair, {{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 4 *Stone lanterns - from {{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 4 to {{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125|1=Kaei}} 2 Yamadaijinjya Haiden.jpg|Haiden Yamadaijinjya Komainu.jpg|Komainu Yamadaijinjya Toro.jpg|Stone lantern === Local information === ===Location=== Uchihata-chō 3619, Kishiwada, Osaka ===Access=== *From {{ill|lt=Kumeda Station|Q857003|WD=Q857003|1=Kumeda Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Hanwa Line|Q1154800|WD=Q1154800|1=Hanwa Line}}, take bus and get off at Sawamine *10 minutes from {{ill|lt=Kishiwada-Izumi Interchange|Q11051119|WD=Q11051119|1=Kishiwada-Izumi interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Hanwa Expressway|Q1872998|WD=Q1872998|1=Hanwa Expressway}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural properties|Q11440963|WD=Q11440963|1=List of Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural properties}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province|Q11417778|WD=Q11417778|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province}} === External links === * [https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/site/kishiwada-side/yamadaijinja.html Yamadai Shrine] - Its honden and other structures are designated {{ill|lt=tangible cultural properties|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}}. === History === In the early {{ill|lt=8th century|Q8086|WD=Q8086|1=8th century}}, in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Monmu|Q357246|WD=Q357246|1=Emperor Monmu}}, a shrine hall of 7 bays was built with a small corner width of 5 {{ill|lt=ken|Q3195007|WD=Q3195007|1=Ken (unit)}}, and later in the age of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, {{ill|lt=Gyōki|Q1136664|WD=Q1136664|1=Gyōki}} petitioned and temple halls and shrine buildings were constructed. The site was later destroyed by war fire in the {{ill|lt=Ōnin|Q1143903|WD=Q1143903|1=Ōnin}} era, but in {{ill|lt=1595|Q6782|WD=Q6782|1=1595}} ({{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 3) a two-by-four-bay shrine hall was rebuilt. It was an Engishiki inner shrine and once had a temple-shrine of this sanctuary called Chōkō-ji, but was separated by {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}. from {{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 4 to {{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125|1=Kaei}} 2 Yamadaijinjya Haiden.jpg|Haiden Yamadaijinjya Komainu.jpg|Komainu Yamadaijinjya Toro.jpg|Stone lantern === Local information === ===Location=== Uchihata-chō 3619, Kishiwada, Osaka ===Access=== *From {{ill|lt=Kumeda Station|Q857003|WD=Q857003|1=Kumeda Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Hanwa Line|Q1154800|WD=Q1154800|1=Hanwa Line}}, take bus and get off at Sawamine *10 minutes from {{ill|lt=Kishiwada-Izumi Interchange|Q11051119|WD=Q11051119|1=Kishiwada-Izumi interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Hanwa Expressway|Q1872998|WD=Q1872998|1=Hanwa Expressway}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural properties|Q11440963|WD=Q11440963|1=List of Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural properties}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province|Q11417778|WD=Q11417778|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province}} === External links === * [https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/site/kishiwada-side/yamadaijinja.html Yamadai Shrine] - Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kishiwada|Q740456|WD=Q740456|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}}Uchihata-chō 3619 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}、{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |神格 = |創建年 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |札所等 = |例祭 = Winter shrine festival (February)
Grand annual festival (October) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Yamadai Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yamadai Shrine''' (やまだいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kishiwada|Q740456|WD=Q740456|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Its honden and other structures are designated {{ill|lt=tangible cultural properties|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}}. === History === In the early {{ill|lt=8th century|Q8086|WD=Q8086|1=8th century}}, in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Monmu|Q357246|WD=Q357246|1=Emperor Monmu}}, a shrine hall of 7 bays was built with a small corner width of 5 {{ill|lt=ken|Q3195007|WD=Q3195007|1=Ken (unit)}}, and later in the age of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, {{ill|lt=Gyōki|Q1136664|WD=Q1136664|1=Gyōki}} petitioned and temple halls and shrine buildings were constructed. The site was later destroyed by war fire in the {{ill|lt=Ōnin|Q1143903|WD=Q1143903|1=Ōnin}} era, but in {{ill|lt=1595|Q6782|WD=Q6782|1=1595}} ({{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 3) a two-by-four-bay shrine hall was rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 6), its thatched roof was repaired.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://kamnavi.jp/en/izumi/yamadai.htm |publisher=Kannabi|title=Yamadai Shrine|accessdate=2020-06-20}}. It was an Engishiki inner shrine and once had a temple-shrine of this sanctuary called Chōkō-ji, but was separated by {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|lt=village shrine|1=Shakaku}}, and in Meiji 44 it merged Yaoyorozu Shrine and Kijima ShrineLocal information signboard. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties / Natural monuments === In {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477|1=1973}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 48), three items were designated cultural properties by Osaka Prefecture. *Honden - built in 1595. {{ill|lt=Japanese cypress|Q917187|WD=Q917187|1=Chamaecyparis obtusa}} bark roof, with chidori {{ill|lt=hafu gable|Q4881511|WD=Q4881511|1=Bargeboard}}. *Stone komainu - one pair, {{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 4 *Stone lanterns - from {{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 4 to {{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125|1=Kaei}} 2 Yamadaijinjya Haiden.jpg|Haiden Yamadaijinjya Komainu.jpg|Komainu Yamadaijinjya Toro.jpg|Stone lantern === Local information === ===Location=== Uchihata-chō 3619, Kishiwada, Osaka ===Access=== *From {{ill|lt=Kumeda Station|Q857003|WD=Q857003|1=Kumeda Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Hanwa Line|Q1154800|WD=Q1154800|1=Hanwa Line}}, take bus and get off at Sawamine *10 minutes from {{ill|lt=Kishiwada-Izumi Interchange|Q11051119|WD=Q11051119|1=Kishiwada-Izumi interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Hanwa Expressway|Q1872998|WD=Q1872998|1=Hanwa Expressway}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural properties|Q11440963|WD=Q11440963|1=List of Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural properties}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province|Q11417778|WD=Q11417778|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province}} === External links === * [https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/site/kishiwada-side/yamadaijinja.html Yamadai Shrine] - Grand annual festival (October) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Yamadai Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yamadai Shrine''' (やまだいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kishiwada|Q740456|WD=Q740456|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Its honden and other structures are designated {{ill|lt=tangible cultural properties|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}}. === History === In the early {{ill|lt=8th century|Q8086|WD=Q8086|1=8th century}}, in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Monmu|Q357246|WD=Q357246|1=Emperor Monmu}}, a shrine hall of 7 bays was built with a small corner width of 5 {{ill|lt=ken|Q3195007|WD=Q3195007|1=Ken (unit)}}, and later in the age of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, {{ill|lt=Gyōki|Q1136664|WD=Q1136664|1=Gyōki}} petitioned and temple halls and shrine buildings were constructed. The site was later destroyed by war fire in the {{ill|lt=Ōnin|Q1143903|WD=Q1143903|1=Ōnin}} era, but in {{ill|lt=1595|Q6782|WD=Q6782|1=1595}} ({{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 3) a two-by-four-bay shrine hall was rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 6), its thatched roof was repaired.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://kamnavi.jp/en/izumi/yamadai.htm |publisher=Kannabi|title=Yamadai Shrine|accessdate=2020-06-20}}. It was an Engishiki inner shrine and once had a temple-shrine of this sanctuary called Chōkō-ji, but was separated by {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|lt=village shrine|1=Shakaku}}, and in Meiji 44 it merged Yaoyorozu Shrine and Kijima ShrineLocal information signboard. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties / Natural monuments === In {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477|1=1973}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 48), three items were designated cultural properties by Osaka Prefecture. *Honden - built in 1595. {{ill|lt=Japanese cypress|Q917187|WD=Q917187|1=Chamaecyparis obtusa}} bark roof, with chidori {{ill|lt=hafu gable|Q4881511|WD=Q4881511|1=Bargeboard}}. *Stone komainu - one pair, {{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 4 *Stone lanterns - Grand annual festival (October) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Yamadai Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yamadai Shrine''' (やまだいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kishiwada|Q740456|WD=Q740456|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Its honden and other structures are designated {{ill|lt=tangible cultural properties|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}}. === History === In the early {{ill|lt=8th century|Q8086|WD=Q8086|1=8th century}}, in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Monmu|Q357246|WD=Q357246|1=Emperor Monmu}}, a shrine hall of 7 bays was built with a small corner width of 5 {{ill|lt=ken|Q3195007|WD=Q3195007|1=Ken (unit)}}, and later in the age of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, {{ill|lt=Gyōki|Q1136664|WD=Q1136664|1=Gyōki}} petitioned and temple halls and shrine buildings were constructed. The site was later destroyed by war fire in the {{ill|lt=Ōnin|Q1143903|WD=Q1143903|1=Ōnin}} era, but in {{ill|lt=1595|Q6782|WD=Q6782|1=1595}} ({{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 3) a two-by-four-bay shrine hall was rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 6), its thatched roof was repaired.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://kamnavi.jp/en/izumi/yamadai.htm |publisher=Kannabi|title=Yamadai Shrine|accessdate=2020-06-20}}. It was an Engishiki inner shrine and once had a temple-shrine of this sanctuary called Chōkō-ji, but was separated by {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|lt=village shrine|1=Shakaku}}, and in Meiji 44 it merged Yaoyorozu Shrine and Kijima ShrineLocal information signboard. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties / Natural monuments === In {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477|1=1973}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 48), three items were designated cultural properties by Osaka Prefecture. *Honden - built in 1595. {{ill|lt=Japanese cypress|Q917187|WD=Q917187|1=Chamaecyparis obtusa}} bark roof, with chidori {{ill|lt=hafu gable|Q4881511|WD=Q4881511|1=Bargeboard}}. *Stone komainu - one pair, {{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 4 *Stone lanterns - from {{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 4 to {{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125|1=Kaei}} 2 Yamadaijinjya Haiden.jpg|Haiden Yamadaijinjya Komainu.jpg|Komainu Yamadaijinjya Toro.jpg|Stone lantern === Local information === ===Location=== Uchihata-chō 3619, Kishiwada, Osaka ===Access=== *From {{ill|lt=Kumeda Station|Q857003|WD=Q857003|1=Kumeda Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Hanwa Line|Q1154800|WD=Q1154800|1=Hanwa Line}}, take bus and get off at Sawamine *10 minutes from {{ill|lt=Kishiwada-Izumi Interchange|Q11051119|WD=Q11051119|1=Kishiwada-Izumi interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Hanwa Expressway|Q1872998|WD=Q1872998|1=Hanwa Expressway}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural properties|Q11440963|WD=Q11440963|1=List of Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural properties}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province|Q11417778|WD=Q11417778|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province}} === External links === * [https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/site/kishiwada-side/yamadaijinja.html Yamadai Shrine] - from {{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 4 to {{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125|1=Kaei}} 2 Yamadaijinjya Haiden.jpg|Haiden Yamadaijinjya Komainu.jpg|Komainu Yamadaijinjya Toro.jpg|Stone lantern === Local information === ===Location=== Uchihata-chō 3619, Kishiwada, Osaka ===Access=== *From {{ill|lt=Kumeda Station|Q857003|WD=Q857003|1=Kumeda Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Hanwa Line|Q1154800|WD=Q1154800|1=Hanwa Line}}, take bus and get off at Sawamine *10 minutes from {{ill|lt=Kishiwada-Izumi Interchange|Q11051119|WD=Q11051119|1=Kishiwada-Izumi interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Hanwa Expressway|Q1872998|WD=Q1872998|1=Hanwa Expressway}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural properties|Q11440963|WD=Q11440963|1=List of Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural properties}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province|Q11417778|WD=Q11417778|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province}} === External links === * [https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/site/kishiwada-side/yamadaijinja.html Yamadai Shrine] - Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kishiwada|Q740456|WD=Q740456|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}}Uchihata-chō 3619 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}、{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |神格 = |創建年 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |札所等 = |例祭 = Winter shrine festival (February)
Grand annual festival (October) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Yamadai Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yamadai Shrine''' (やまだいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kishiwada|Q740456|WD=Q740456|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Its honden and other structures are designated {{ill|lt=tangible cultural properties|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}}. === History === In the early {{ill|lt=8th century|Q8086|WD=Q8086|1=8th century}}, in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Monmu|Q357246|WD=Q357246|1=Emperor Monmu}}, a shrine hall of 7 bays was built with a small corner width of 5 {{ill|lt=ken|Q3195007|WD=Q3195007|1=Ken (unit)}}, and later in the age of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|WD=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, {{ill|lt=Gyōki|Q1136664|WD=Q1136664|1=Gyōki}} petitioned and temple halls and shrine buildings were constructed. The site was later destroyed by war fire in the {{ill|lt=Ōnin|Q1143903|WD=Q1143903|1=Ōnin}} era, but in {{ill|lt=1595|Q6782|WD=Q6782|1=1595}} ({{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 3) a two-by-four-bay shrine hall was rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 6), its thatched roof was repaired.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://kamnavi.jp/en/izumi/yamadai.htm |publisher=Kannabi|title=Yamadai Shrine|accessdate=2020-06-20}}. It was an Engishiki inner shrine and once had a temple-shrine of this sanctuary called Chōkō-ji, but was separated by {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5) it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|lt=village shrine|1=Shakaku}}, and in Meiji 44 it merged Yaoyorozu Shrine and Kijima ShrineLocal information signboard. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Cultural properties / Natural monuments === In {{ill|lt=1973|Q2477|WD=Q2477|1=1973}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 48), three items were designated cultural properties by Osaka Prefecture. *Honden - built in 1595. {{ill|lt=Japanese cypress|Q917187|WD=Q917187|1=Chamaecyparis obtusa}} bark roof, with chidori {{ill|lt=hafu gable|Q4881511|WD=Q4881511|1=Bargeboard}}. *Stone komainu - one pair, {{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 4 *Stone lanterns - from {{ill|lt=Hōreki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 4 to {{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125|1=Kaei}} 2 Yamadaijinjya Haiden.jpg|Haiden Yamadaijinjya Komainu.jpg|Komainu Yamadaijinjya Toro.jpg|Stone lantern === Local information === ===Location=== Uchihata-chō 3619, Kishiwada, Osaka ===Access=== *From {{ill|lt=Kumeda Station|Q857003|WD=Q857003|1=Kumeda Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Hanwa Line|Q1154800|WD=Q1154800|1=Hanwa Line}}, take bus and get off at Sawamine *10 minutes from {{ill|lt=Kishiwada-Izumi Interchange|Q11051119|WD=Q11051119|1=Kishiwada-Izumi interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Hanwa Expressway|Q1872998|WD=Q1872998|1=Hanwa Expressway}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural properties|Q11440963|WD=Q11440963|1=List of Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural properties}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province|Q11417778|WD=Q11417778|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province}} === External links === * [https://www.city.kishiwada.osaka.jp/site/kishiwada-side/yamadaijinja.html Yamadai Shrine] -
